rulings.Case Title: LONNETTE ROBINSON ET AL VS. LARUE PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dissolving Partnership And Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER KERRY BOWSER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER DISSOLVING PARTNERSHIP AND APPOINTING RECEIVER is continued to March 17, 2023 for the moving party to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers no later than March 1, 2023 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (Opposition and reply received). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SCHMITT VS. TRANSITO RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the cause of action for unlawful detainer. Plaintiff failed to comply with Regulation 12.14(b)(8). Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to file a complaint for ejectment (a cause of action suggested in the opposition) and obtain appropriate 30-day summons. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file and serve an amended complaint. If Plaintiff fails to file an amended complaint, defendant may obtain a judgment in this action per the order sustaining the demurrer to the cause of action for unlawful detainer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
